invalid_review_attempts.command_shape: claude -p read-only review executed before full-access environment could read local auth state

parsed_review.summary: CP00-157 adds descriptor-only Master Data service request normalization, prechecks, primary/secondary workflow descriptors, review/approval/blocked routing, idempotency/lock descriptors, and H04/C04 packets while preserving the CP00-156 foundation and all no-write attestations. Boundaries hold (no DB/product writes, no permission evaluation, no audit append, no API/network), main outcome paths are tested, and the contract/registry/validator wiring is internally consistent. Findings are quality/clarity items (untested negative branches, static 'checked' evidence, naming-vs-behavior) and do not block pack or goal closeout.

boundary_assessment.notes: permission_precheck/audit_hint_precheck are presence-only checks (descriptor_ref != null), not evaluation; action_preview is built via createMasterDataRecord which returns a frozen synthetic descriptor (creates_database_rows:false asserted and tested); lock_key is declared-only with acquires_runtime_lock:false. Main happy/review/approval/blocked paths are tested.

findings.title: Several service-level blocked-claim routes have no test coverage
findings.evidence: MASTER_DATA_SERVICE_BOUNDARY.blocked_claims enumerates unsupported_service_operation, tenant_scope_missing, tenant_boundary_mismatch, and idempotency_key_required, plus the defensive service_factory_descriptor_error path in executeMasterDataServiceWorkflow. model.test.js and validate-rp04-master-data-contract.mjs only exercise relationship_direction_error, client_group_leakage, missing_matter_trace, permission_precheck_required, and audit_hint_precheck_required. The new service-only branches (precheck tenant/operation/idempotency logic in service.js:executeMasterDataServicePrechecks) are validated by inspection only.
findings.recommended_action: Add service tests: an unsupported operation, a request missing tenant_id, an explicit expected_tenant_id/payload tenant_id mismatch, and (if isolable) idempotency_key_required, asserting outcome:'blocked' and the specific blocked_claims.
findings.post_review_disposition: fixed: added unsupported operation, missing tenant, tenant mismatch, idempotency, and factory/error route-adjacent blocked path tests in packages/master-data/test/model.test.js

findings.title: Precheck 'checked' field is a static constant and overstates executed checks
findings.evidence: executeMasterDataServicePrechecks always returns checked: Object.freeze(MASTER_DATA_SERVICE_BOUNDARY.prechecks) (all six). The validateMasterDataRecord delegation (source of state_transition_enforcement) is gated by 'if (operationDefinition && normalized.model_type && normalized.payload)', so for an unsupported operation that record-level enforcement is skipped while 'checked' still claims state_transition_enforcement and matter-record checks ran. This is descriptor evidence that does not reflect actual execution.
findings.recommended_action: Either derive 'checked' from the prechecks actually run, or rename/document the field as a declared-capability list distinct from an executed-checks attestation so the H04/C04 evidence is not misread.
findings.post_review_disposition: fixed: executeMasterDataServicePrechecks now returns executed checked entries and declared_prechecks separately

findings.title: 'execute*' verbs imply side effects but functions are pure descriptor builders (production_ready/integration risk)
findings.evidence: executeMasterDataServiceWorkflow / executeMasterDataServicePrechecks / executeMasterData*Workflow only return frozen descriptors (synthetic_only:true, writes_product_state:false). The verb 'execute' plus production_ready_flag 'master_data_service_logic_boundary_verified' could lead a future CP00-158 consumer to wire these expecting real persistence/enforcement.
findings.recommended_action: Add a doc/JSDoc note (or descriptor-suffixed alias) clarifying these are preview builders, and ensure CP00-158 runtime wiring does not treat the descriptor return as an applied effect. Mitigated today by the embedded attestation flags.
findings.post_review_disposition: fixed: added descriptor-builder note in service.js and README boundary text

findings.title: state_transition_enforcement realized only as lifecycle-status validity, not a transition matrix
findings.evidence: The contract/intent reference 'state transition routing' and a state_transition_enforcement precheck, but there is no from->to transition logic; invalid_lifecycle_status comes solely from validateMasterDataRecord's status-value check. No current->next transition is evaluated.
findings.recommended_action: Clarify in README/contract that S06-S10 covers status-value validity only, deferring true transition enforcement to the CP00-158 lock/persistence tail, so the precheck name does not overstate behavior.
findings.post_review_disposition: fixed: contract and README clarify lifecycle-status-only state transition scope with matrix enforcement deferred

findings.title: Declared retry_behavior/rollback_behavior are not exercised by any test
findings.evidence: service_logic.retry_behavior ('same_idempotency_key_returns_same_descriptor_shape') and rollback_behavior ('descriptor_noop') are asserted in the contract. Descriptors are deterministic by construction (no Date/random), but no test calls the workflow twice with identical input to confirm idempotent descriptor shape.
findings.recommended_action: Add a determinism test invoking executeMasterDataServiceWorkflow twice with the same request and deepEqual-asserting the returned descriptor (and stable idempotency_key/lock_key).
findings.post_review_disposition: fixed: added deterministic retry/rollback descriptor deepEqual test

parsed_review.p3_disposition: all three P3 findings fixed through documentation or deterministic descriptor tests
